title: Aequatorium tuestae
taxonomy: Aequatorium tuestae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the genus Aequatorium, which is native to Ecuador. The plant is classified as a type of herb or shrub, with a growth habit that is not well-documented. The species is relatively rare and has not been extensively studied. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.

intro: Aequatorium tuestae, commonly known as Tuesta's flower, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aequatorium tuestae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aequatorium tuestae is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular remedies for colds, sore throats, and minor infections.
